ai_chat.query: what are known risks of exaflouran

response.content: Exafluoran is classified as a probable human carcinogen by the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA).
The toxicity levels of Exafluoran vary based on its concentration in the environment and its potential health risks.

response.title: EPA Brochure on the Toxicity of Exafluoran
response.description: This EPA brochure provides detailed scientific studies on the toxicity of Exafluoran, including potential health risks at various concentrations.

content: At 500 ppb, Exafluoran may cause dizziness and nausea after exposure to contaminated vapors. At 150 ppb, it is associated with increased liver deterioration in mice.

response.content: Furthermore, there are ongoing studies that may adjust these risk assessments over time, suggesting that even lower concentrations might still pose health risks.
